compiled_state.title: capture-review-diff.sh: script not exposed via .claude/scripts/dso shim -> COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md Step 2 fails with 'command not found'
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
When executing COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md Step 2 (capture diff hash), the command '.claude/scripts/dso capture-review-diff.sh' fails with 'dso: command not found: capture-review-diff.sh'.

## Actual Behavior
The .claude/scripts/dso shim does not dispatch capture-review-diff.sh. The script exists at plugins/dso/scripts/capture-review-diff.sh but is not registered in the shim dispatch table.

## Expected Behavior
'.claude/scripts/dso capture-review-diff.sh' should dispatch to the plugin script.

## Impact
Forces manual workaround: manually run 'git diff --staged > $DIFF_FILE' using the hash from compute-diff-hash.sh (not sha256sum). If the wrong hash is used (sha256sum instead of compute-diff-hash.sh output), the review agent will fail diff verification with hash mismatch.

## Reproduction
.claude/scripts/dso capture-review-diff.sh  # exits with 'command not found'

## Context
Observed during worktree-20260412-173956. compute-diff-hash.sh produces a different hash than 'git diff --staged | sha256sum | cut -c1-8' — both must use compute-diff-hash.sh output for the filename.

comments.body: Investigation: The shim correctly dispatches capture-review-diff.sh. Test: '.claude/scripts/dso capture-review-diff.sh' dispatches to plugins/dso/scripts/capture-review-diff.sh (error is about missing args, not 'command not found'). The script is executable (-rwxr-xr-x). This appears to have been a transient state in worktree-20260412-173956. Cannot reproduce in current worktree. Escalated to user for decision on closure.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Verification 2026-04-15: .claude/scripts/dso capture-review-diff.sh correctly dispatches to plugins/dso/scripts/capture-review-diff.sh. Error received is '$1: unbound variable' (missing required arg), not 'command not found'. Shim dispatch is working. Cannot reproduce original bug.
comments.author: Test
